items.q: Why is a cold winter not evidence against climate change?
items.correct: Climate refers to long-term averages, not individual weather events.
items.misconception: It is evidence - if it gets cold sometimes, warming must be wrong.
items.random_wrong: Cold winters are caused by warming.
items.why: Students conflate weather and climate. Climate is the LONG-TERM pattern (decades+); weather is short-term. Global warming refers to average trends - it allows individual cold events even as overall temperatures rise.

items.q: Why is atmospheric nitrogen unavailable to most plants directly?
items.correct: N2 has a strong triple bond that most organisms cannot break.
items.misconception: Because plants cannot reach high enough to access it.
items.why: Students miss the chemistry. Despite 78% of air being N2, the molecule's triple bond is energetically costly to break. Only specialised nitrogen-fixing bacteria (and lightning, industrial Haber process) can convert it to usable forms.

items.q: Why are invasive species often more harmful than native species?
items.correct: They lack natural predators or competitors in their new environment, so they spread unchecked.
items.misconception: They are inherently more aggressive than native species.
items.random_wrong: They are larger than native species.
items.why: Students attribute success to species traits. The key factor is the ABSENCE OF CONTROLS - the same species may be ordinary in its native range, where co-evolved predators and competitors keep it in check.

items.q: Hardin's 'Tragedy of the Commons' was published in:
items.why: Correct answer: 1968. “1962” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: The Brundtland definition of sustainable development appeared in:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1987. “1972” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Year of the Rio Earth Summit?
items.why: The real figure is 1992 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
